title: Sjaak
text: Home * Engines * Sjaak Sjaak [1] , (TrojanKnight) a private chess, Antichess , and later Atomic Chess playing program by Ronald de Man , registered under the handle TrojanKnight [2] at FICS [3] [4] . Sjaak has a parallel SMP search [5] , and was test bed of Ronald de Man's Syzygy Bases [6] . Contents 1 Namesake 2 See also 3 External Links 3.1 Chess Engine 3.2 Misc 4 References Namesake [ edit ] Sjaak by Evert Glebbeek [7] See also [ edit ] Sjakk External Links [ edit ] Chess Engine [ edit ] February 2019 – Chronatog - Golden Era of Atomic Chess (1995-1999) Misc [ edit ] Sjaak from Wikipedia Sjaak from Wikipedia.nl (Dutch) ICGA: Losing Chess by Guy Haworth References [ edit ] ↑ Sjaak - Dutch given name derived from Jacob , pronunciation similar to Schaak ↑ Re: AEGON 97/ 1st round: HIARCS lost by Ronald de Man , rgcc , April 25 1997 ↑ FICS Statistics - April 2004, Best Ratings - Computer List ↑ February 2019 – Chronatog - Golden Era of Atomic Chess (1995-1999) ↑ Re: back to the Komodo SMP issue by Marcel van Kervinck , CCC , July 06, 2013 ↑ New 6-piece tablebases by Ronald de Man , CCC , April 01, 2013 ↑ Re: New Spartan-Chess engine: Sjaak by Vladimir Xern , CCC , January 21, 2011 Up one Level
